options.title: Chemistry and Biochemistry
options.description: Chemistry 20AH or 20BH may be substituted for 20A or 20B respectively.
courses: CHEM 30A - Organic Chemistry I: Structure and Reactivity

options.title: Physics
courses: PHYSICS 1A - Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Mechanics
PHYSICS 1B - Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Oscillations, Waves, Electric and Magnetic Fields
PHYSICS 1C - Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Electrodynamics, Optics, and Special Relativity
PHYSICS 4BL - Physics Laboratory for Scientists and Engineers: Electricity and Magnetism

json_data.title: The Major
json_data.description: Complete four required courses, one course from Chemistry C172, C180, or C181, and 4 elective units as follows:

options.title: Required
courses: CHEM 113A - Physical Chemistry: Introduction to Quantum Mechanics

options.title: Chemistry C172 or C180 or C181
Chemistry Elective
courses: CHEM C174 - Inorganic and Metalorganic Laboratory Methods
CHEM C176 - Group Theory and Applications to Inorganic Chemistry

options.title: Required
courses: MAT SCI 104 - Science of Engineering Materials
MAT SCI 110 - Introduction to Materials Characterization A (Crystal Structure, Nanostructures, and X-Ray Scattering)
MAT SCI 110L - Introduction to Materials Characterization A Laboratory
MAT SCI 131 - Diffusion and Diffusion-Controlled Reactions

options.title: Materials Science and Engineering 121 or 150 or 160
courses: MAT SCI 121 - Materials Science of Semiconductors
MAT SCI 160 - Introduction to Ceramics and Glasses

options.title: Materials Science Elective
courses: MAT SCI C111 - Introduction to Materials Characterization B (Electron Microscopy)
MAT SCI 121 - Materials Science of Semiconductors
MAT SCI 122 - Principles of Electronic Materials Processing
MAT SCI 132 - Structure and Properties of Metallic Alloys
MAT SCI 160 - Introduction to Ceramics and Glasses
MAT SCI CM180 - Introduction to Biomaterials

options.title: Laboratory
courses: MAT SCI 121L - Materials Science of Semiconductors Laboratory
MAT SCI 131L - Diffusion and Diffusion-Controlled Reactions Laboratory

options.title: Chemistry
options.description: Chemistry 20AH or 20BH may be substituted for 20A or 20B respectively.
courses: CHEM 30A - Organic Chemistry I: Structure and Reactivity
CHEM 30B - Organic Chemistry II: Reactivity, Synthesis, and Spectroscopy
CHEM 30C - Organic Chemistry III: Reactivity, Synthesis, and Biomolecules

options.title: Physics
courses: PHYSICS 1A - Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Mechanics
PHYSICS 1B - Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Oscillations, Waves, Electric and Magnetic Fields
PHYSICS 1C - Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Electrodynamics, Optics, and Special Relativity
PHYSICS 4BL - Physics Laboratory for Scientists and Engineers: Electricity and Magnetism

options.title: The Major
options.description: Complete the following five courses and 4 elective units:

options.title: Chemistry and Biochemistry
courses: CHEM 113A - Physical Chemistry: Introduction to Quantum Mechanics

options.title: Materials Science and Engineering
courses: MAT SCI 104 - Science of Engineering Materials
MAT SCI 110 - Introduction to Materials Characterization A (Crystal Structure, Nanostructures, and X-Ray Scattering)
MAT SCI 110L - Introduction to Materials Characterization A Laboratory

options.title: Laboratory
courses: MAT SCI 121L - Materials Science of Semiconductors Laboratory
MAT SCI 131L - Diffusion and Diffusion-Controlled Reactions Laboratory

options.title: Undergraduate Seminar
courses: CHEM 193A - Journal Club Seminars: UC LEADS and MARC
CHEM 193B - Journal Club Seminars: Chemistry and Biochemistry

options.title: Research
courses: CHEM 196A - Research Apprenticeship in Chemistry and Biochemistry
CHEM 196B - Research Apprenticeship in Chemistry and Biochemistry
CHEM 199 - Directed Research in Chemistry and Biochemistry

json_data.title: Computing Specialization
json_data.description: Complete the following two courses and one elective:

options.title: Program in Computing Elective
courses: COMPTNG 15 - Introduction to Lisp and Symbolic Computation
COMPTNG 20A - Principles of Java Language with Applications

options.title: Computational Chemistry
courses: CHEM C126A - Computational Methods for Chemists
CHEM C145 - Theoretical and Computational Organic Chemistry
COM SCI C121 - Probabilistic Models in Computational Genomics
